key_insight: An AI-guided discovery pipeline expanded phosphide chemical space and uncovered thousands of stable, previously unreported structures, including a set of new semiconductor candidates.
engineering_relevance: The workflow shows how generative models, machine-learning interatomic potentials, and DFT can be combined to rapidly screen materials for optoelectronic and thermoelectric applications.
method: ICSD-based Wyckoff-site substitution expanded known phosphide structures.
MatterGen generated new candidate structures conditionally.
A domain-finetuned DPA3 potential prefiltered stability before DFT and HSE06 validation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 3,574 previously unreported stable phosphide structures, including 196 semiconductors with HSE06 band gaps between 0 and 3.0 eV, plus 30 optoelectronic and 26 thermoelectric candidates.
watch_item: The results depend on model transferability and downstream DFT validation, so experimental synthesis and property confirmation are still needed.
paper_title: Robust AI-Driven Discovery of Electronic Metal Phosphide Semiconductors
